summary: Stellar Eyes opener, high‑energy jams & epic Truckin’/New Speedway stretch
blurb: The concert bursts open with a soaring 'Eyes Of The World' and rides through tight first‑set highlights like 'Loose Lucy' and 'Might As Well'. A second set packed with Dark Star teases, a monster Truckin’ → New Speedway Boogie sequence, and a rousing encore of 'The Weight' captures the band at peak form.
review: June 17, 1991 at Giants Stadium remains one of the most talked‑about nights of the Hornsby era. Jerry Garcia opened with his voice “at its best” on "Eyes Of The World" and launched into scorching leads that kept the crowd humming all night long. Bruce Hornsby’s piano was a catalyst, injecting fresh energy into every jam—from the dark‑star‑laden interludes to the soaring Truckin’ → New Speedway Boogie stretch that many reviewers call “year‑best”. The rhythm section never missed a beat: Phil Lesh laid down deep, rock‑solid bass bombs, while Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart delivered thunderous, perfectly balanced drumming despite a brief digi‑noise blip at the show’s start. Bob Weir’s rhythm guitar and vocal work shone on "Loose Lucy" and "Might As Well", providing a sturdy counterpoint to Jerry’s lead. The second set flowed seamlessly through "Saint Of Circumstance", "Ship Of Fools", an expansive Dark Star jam, and wrapped up with a rapturous encore of "The Weight"—a performance that feels both legendary and technically tight. Across dozens of audience, matrix and soundboard sources the consensus is clear: this show is a high‑energy, tightly executed showcase of the 1991 Dead at the height of their improvisational powers.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All twelve analyzed recordings carry a positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the show as legendary, magical and high‑energy. Multiple sources highlight the same standout moments—Eyes opener, Dark Star teases, Truckin’/New Speedway Boogie stretch—and commend Jerry’s vocals, Hornsby’s piano, and the tight rhythm section. No negative commentary appears, just minor technical notes, so a strong positive adjustment is justified.

best_recording.reason: Crisp, well‑balanced soundboard mix that captures every instrument clearly; minor digi‑noise burst is the only flaw and it doesn’t detract from the overall listening experience.

key_highlights: Opening "Eyes Of The World" sets a soaring tone.
Dark Star teases weave through both sets, keeping the jam momentum high.
"Truckin' → New Speedway Boogie" stretch is hailed as one of the year’s best.
Horny‑era piano work adds fresh color throughout, especially on "Loose Lucy" and the second‑set jams.

band_performance.Jerry: Vocals described as at his best; guitar tone and solos were energetic, scorching, and perfectly locked in with Hornsby’s piano.
band_performance.Phil: Tight, rock‑solid bass work with deep bombs on Truckin'; overall solid foundation despite occasional mix burying.
band_performance.Bob: Strong rhythm‑guitar chops and vocal contributions; highlighted on Loose Lucy and synergistic interplay with Jerry.
band_performance.Mickey: Powerful, thunderous drumming that drove the set; praised for balance even with heavy bass pressure.
band_performance.Billy: Heavy, well‑balanced playing that slayed throughout; contributed to the high‑energy feel despite a brief digi‑noise burst at the start.
